regime_justification: USDJPY at 159.38, close to April intervention threshold of 160, with BOJ officials reaffirming commitment to further hikes while stopping short of near-term signals; spreads wide but pace slowing; intervention risk elevated.

domino_stage.label: Pressure Building
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY trending toward 160 watch line (18 pips below), BOJ rhetoric shifting hawkish (Himino/Ueda comments), JGB 10Y at 2.71% (near 29-year highs), oil rising on Iran conflict, but no imminent trigger yet.

1w.label: Unlikely
1w.driver: No imminent BOJ meeting (next Jun 15-16), USDJPY holding below 160, vol subdued at ~7-8%, MOVE 75, VIX 16.3; intervention possible but not cocked.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Applied unwind prior; would otherwise grade D. BOJ meeting Jun 15-16 inside window, Himino/Ueda comments signal growing hawkish tilt, USDJPY creep continuing, Iran tension keeping oil/inflation elevated.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: BOJ meeting (Jun 15-16) is primary catalyst; market pricing ~50% chance of hike, spread at 177 bps still wide, JGB at 2.71% near cycle highs, setup fragile but resolution path visible.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: BOJ meeting outcome will have clarified path by late June; either intervention/hike relieves pressure or carry resumes; oil expected to stabilize if Iran talks progress.

bottom_line: Stage 1 (Pressure Building): USDJPY at 159.38, 18 pips from 160 intervention threshold. BOJ Deputy Gov Himino reiterated hike commitment May 26; Gov Ueda warned on oil-driven inflation but gave no timing signal. JGB 10Y at 2.71% (29-year high territory). Spread 177 bps, carry-to-vol ~23× still attractive. Oil $90-91 on Iran strikes. Next BOJ meeting Jun 15-16 is key catalyst. Grade 1w/2w/3w/4w: D/C/C/D. Watchful on 2-3 week horizon around BOJ meeting; applied unwind prior to 2w (would be D without it). No trigger imminent but setup tightening.

stack.note: 18 pips below 160 intervention threshold; 5d pace +0.48%, well below April's panic; 1m low 158.85, high 159.62.
Subdued; exact quote unavailable but sources cite low vol environment; well below April spike levels.
Snapping 5-session decline on Iran strikes; Fed hike priced ~50% by Dec per sources.
Pulled back from 2.8% May 18 (29-year high); Ueda warned on oil/inflation May 27, no hike signal given.
Widening slightly; carry-to-vol ~23.6× (177/7.5) remains highly attractive, funding carry pressure.
Rebounding on fresh US-Iran strikes in Hormuz; ¥/bbl = 14,404 (159.38×90.35), import inflation elevated.
Calm; well below 100 threshold, no rates-vol regime change signal.
Subdued; equity vol low, risk-on bias despite geopolitical noise.
Down on Iran strikes, risk-off pullback; -$34k YoY, symptomatic of liquidity drain in high-beta assets.
Off record highs; tech-led pullback (SoftBank -2%, Advantest -2.9%); tracking oil/BOJ uncertainty.

trigger_map.note: USDJPY 159.38, 18 pips from 160; MOF intervened Apr 30/May 1 at 160+; finance ministry data due May 30 to confirm ~$30B spent; rhetoric active but no rate-checks reported today.
Next meeting Jun 15-16 (18 days out). Himino May 26: 'will continue to raise policy rate'; Ueda May 27 warned oil inflation but gave no timing. Market pricing ~50% Dec hike per sources; not imminent.

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: Currently 2.71%; May 18 touched 2.8% (29-year high). Cycle high is ~2.8%; needs sustained break to signal unanchored expectations.
WTI $90.35, fresh US strikes on Iran drones May 28; Hormuz negotiations stalled; oil rally paused but tail risk elevated. ¥/bbl 14,404 below Apr peak but rising.
Currently 75; no rates-vol regime change, calm conditions.

narrative.p1: Overnight: USDJPY drifted lower to 159.38 (-0.09%), pulling back modestly after reports of fresh US strikes on Iranian drone sites in Hormuz. Oil rebounded +2% to $90.35 on renewed conflict, while US 10Y rose to 4.48% (snapping 5-session decline). BOJ Gov Ueda warned May 27 about oil-driven inflation but stopped short of signaling a Jun hike. Deputy Gov Himino reiterated May 26 that the BOJ 'will continue to raise the policy rate,' keeping hawkish expectations alive but timing uncertain. JGB 10Y at 2.71%, down 1 bp from prior session but still near 29-year high territory (touched 2.8% May 18). Nikkei fell -0.47% on tech weakness. No material change in stage; remain Stage 1 (Pressure Building).
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP spread 177 bps (+3 bps), JGB 10Y 2.71%, USDJPY 1M vol ~7.5% (subdued). Carry-to-vol ~23.6×, still deeply attractive and funding carry flows. USDJPY 18 pips below 160 intervention line; 5d pace +0.48% (orderly, well below April's multi-percent spikes that triggered MOF). Distance from 60d mean: neutral to slightly stretched. Stage 1: BOJ rhetoric shifting (Himino/Ueda), JGB yields elevated, USDJPY creeping higher, oil pressure from Iran, but no imminent trigger. 1-week grade D: no BOJ meeting, USDJPY holding below 160, vol/MOVE/VIX all calm, intervention possible but not cocked. 2-week grade C: applied unwind prior; would otherwise grade D. BOJ meeting Jun 15-16 is 18 days out (inside 2w+), Himino/Ueda comments signal policy tilt, USDJPY grind continuing, oil elevated—setup is fragile even if no hard catalyst yet. Prior justifies one-notch bump from D to C. 3-week grade C: BOJ meeting is primary catalyst, market pricing ~50% hike odds (per sources citing Dec curve), spread wide, JGB near highs, resolution path visible but risk elevated. 4-week grade D: meeting outcome will clarify path; pressure either relieved or carry resumes.
narrative.p3: BTC $73,460 (-2.3% on Iran strikes), down $34k YoY, symptomatic of sustained liquidity drain in high-beta risk assets. ETF outflows: $1.26B over 6 days in late May (reversing March/April inflows), per sources. BTC remains correlated to risk-on/off (sold on geopolitical flares), not yet decoupled as inflation hedge. Open interest data unavailable but directional flow is net selling. XRP data sparse. For crypto positioning: defensive. The unwind thesis implies rising real rates + stronger yen = tighter global liquidity = crypto headwind. BTC holding $73k is support, but no bid from carry unwind beneficiaries (yen longs). If BOJ hikes Jun 15-16, expect short-term vol spike and possible flush toward $65-70k zone (prior support). If BOJ stands pat, relief rally possible but capped by macro uncertainty. Rotation out of levered long-duration trades (including crypto) is ongoing; late-May outflows confirm the tape.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1w is data-driven calm (D): no meeting, USDJPY below 160, vol subdued. 2w incorporates BOJ meeting setup (Jun 15-16, 18 days out) and elevated geopolitical tail risk (Iran). Applied unwind prior to bump 2w from D to C; the thesis is that BOJ rhetoric + USDJPY grind + oil pressure = fragile setup even without hard catalyst. Grade reflects growing probability of intervention or hawkish surprise inside 2-3 weeks.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Both C (Watchful). 2w is pre-meeting setup risk; 3w includes the Jun 15-16 BOJ decision itself. Market pricing ~50% hike odds per sources. If BOJ hikes, immediate unwind trigger (Stage 3). If BOJ holds but signals accelerated normalization, stays Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention) with elevated risk. 3w sustains C because catalyst is inside the window and outcome distribution is wide.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3w C, 4w D. By week 4 (late June), BOJ meeting outcome will be known. If hike delivered, initial unwind vol spike will have occurred and begun to stabilize (Stage 4→5 transition possible). If no hike, carry pressure resumes and setup decompresses back toward Stage 1 or 0. Oil also expected to stabilize if Iran talks progress (per sources noting peace deal hopes). 4w grade D reflects post-catalyst normalization, lower ambiguity.

watch_list: MOF FX intervention data release (due May 30) — confirms April spend, may include May 27-28 activity if any.
USDJPY daily closes: break above 160 = intervention trigger likely within 24-48 hours.
BOJ speakers ahead of Jun 15-16 meeting (Ueda, Himino, board members) — any shift toward explicit hike signal moves to Stage 2.
Iran-US Hormuz negotiations — breakthrough = oil falls, decompresses ¥/bbl inflation channel; escalation = Stage 2.
JGB 10Y: sustained break above 2.8% (prior cycle high) = unanchored inflation expectations, raises BOJ hike odds and Stage 2 risk.
